The issuer master ITSELF must be bi-temporal ('was-listed / was-active as-of T'), not just the alias edges. As written, every 'as-of T' conclusion is contaminated by knowing who survived to today.

STATISTICAL POWER, NOT JUST CORRECTNESS, BREAKS THE INFO-FLOW LAYER. 'Time is not a constraint' is wall-clock compute -- it does NOT manufacture observations. Conditional/multivariate effective transfer entropy conditioned on index+sector+macro common drivers is data-hungry; a few years of daily bars yields far too few observations for a reliable high-dimensional TE estimate, and finite-sample TE is biased upward exactly where data is thin. The elaborate ADF->MI->Granger->TE->IAAFT->BY-FDR cascade can be procedurally perfect and still emit FDR-survivors that are artifacts of an under-powered, biased estimator -- false rigor. No sampling frequency, minimum-observation gate per pair, or power justification is stated.
overEngineering: SPLINK / FELLEGI-SUNTER PROBABILISTIC RECORD LINKAGE contradicts the doc's own framing. The resolve layer correctly argues the universe is bounded and this is 'gazetteer LINKING, not million-record dedup' -- then specifies Splink (a probabilistic dedup engine built for large, key-less, dirty record sets) for issuer-master construction. A few-thousand-issuer master keyed by LEI/CIK/DART corp_code/ISIN/FIGI resolves by DETERMINISTIC ID joins; probabilistic linkage with TF down-weighting is machinery for a problem the doc itself says doesn't exist here. Drop Splink; keep deterministic joins + the embedding-blocking + closed-set-LLM-with-abstain path for the genuinely ambiguous residue.

THE ENTIRE INFO-FLOW / CAUSAL-DISCOVERY ENGINE is borderline scope-creep for a 2-person 'organize text for a human' system. Its output (directional FDR-surviving lead-lag edges) is the closest thing in the whole design to a trading signal -- the most expensive analytic, the least aligned with the stated GOAL, and the one most likely to mislead. It is correctly phased LAST, but the doc never asks whether it belongs at all; for 2 consumers it reads like a research project bolted onto a knowledge-organization tool. At minimum, gate it behind an explicit 'is this earning its complexity?' decision, not just 'build it last.'

DEBERTA-NLI ENTAILMENT VERIFICATION, self-consistency passes, AND a separate local NLI model are three overlapping faithfulness mechanisms. For 1-2 consumers and Tier-1-dominated high-stakes edges, this is more verification surface area than the consumer count warrants; the doc already gates extraction by salience+credibility, which removes most of the population NLI is meant to catch. Pick one verification layer for high-stakes edges, not a stack of them.

Recursive Leiden FUSED WITH BERTopic(UMAP+HDBSCAN) FUSED WITH c-TF-IDF, then optional LLM label refinement, run on every batch -- the 'agreement = real theme' justification is sound in principle, but running two full independent clustering stacks over the whole graph on a batch cadence for 2 readers is heavy. A single clustering pass with periodic cross-validation against the other method would deliver the same human-facing navigability at a fraction of the compute and tuning burden.
underspecified: Human-review-queue SERVICE LEVEL: no max queue depth, no drain rate, no policy for what happens to mentions stuck unresolved (do they enter the graph provisionally? get dropped? block downstream metrics?). Without this the ER safety valve is undefined and the graph-fragmentation failure mode is unbounded.

POINT-IN-TIME PRICE DATA: Alpaca/CCXT/Upbit price series are split/dividend-adjusted and revised. The doc demands no-lookahead for facts but never addresses that a back-adjusted price series injects future corporate-action knowledge into any historical lead-lag. Need to store adjustment VINTAGE / use as-of-then-unadjusted prices, or the 'event-time series via the bi-temporal layer' guarantee is hollow for the one series the causal analysis depends on most.

KNOWLEDGE-TIME QUANTIZATION vs LAG RESOLUTION: community knowledge_time is set by crawl cadence (you poll 종토방 every N hours). The lead-lag analysis claims to measure information arrival timing, but knowledge_time is quantized to the crawl interval -- at short horizons the crawl cadence blurs or manufactures the very lag k being measured. No crawl frequency is stated relative to the lag horizons of interest.

SOURCE-CREDIBILITY PRIOR: the doc concedes (openQuestions) it has no calibrated taxonomy. This is load-bearing -- it seeds TrustRank teleport, edge weights, AND truth-discovery priors. Three downstream systems consume a number the doc admits it cannot yet define. Needs at least an initial defensible class taxonomy + a calibration/audit loop spec before Phase 2.

INDEPENDENCE / CIB DETECTION mechanism: 'shared-text/synchronized-timing => same source' is trivially defeated by LLM-paraphrased pump content (low text similarity, still coordinated) AND it over-suppresses legitimate corroboration where many users independently quote the SAME DART filing (high text similarity, genuinely the primary source, not coordination). The doc names default-low-independence as safe but never specifies how to distinguish 'echoing a filing' from 'colluding,' which is the actual hard case.

GDELT RELIANCE: the spine leans on GDELT's pre-extracted entities/themes/tone 'to save enormous LLM cost,' but no plan exists for how GDELT's own (English-biased, machine-extracted, frequently noisy) entity/tone tags get reconciled with the Issuer->Security->Listing canonical store or re-credibility-weighted. Imported pre-extraction inherits GDELT's extraction errors directly into the graph.
concreteFixes: SPINE: Keep all-Postgres (the conclusion is correct) but REWRITE the justification. Delete the 'Graphiti erases the rumor trail' claim -- it is verifiably false (Graphiti sets invalid_at/expired_at and preserves superseded edges; it is bi-temporal by design). Rest the decision on the two reasons that actually survive: (a) Postgres is required regardless for resolution/provenance/credibility/cost tables, so Graphiti forces a 2nd engine + cross-engine desync risk; (b) explicit SQL:2011 validity columns are auditable. Also correct the AGE caveat: PG17 (AGE 1.6.0) and PG18 (1.7.0) ARE supported, apache/age Docker images exist, and Azure offers it managed -- 'source-install-only, trails newest PG, no prebuilt image' is outdated.

ER QUEUE: Define an explicit abstain-queue policy. Mentions that abstain enter the graph as a PROVISIONAL/unresolved node tagged low-confidence (never force-merged, never dropped), excluded from importance metrics until resolved, with a bounded queue and an alert when drain-rate < arrival-rate. Add active-learning to shrink the queue (auto-resolve future identical surface forms once a human resolves one). Treat queue-depth and abstain-rate as primary health metrics, as the doc already hints but does not operationalize.

SURVIVORSHIP: Make the issuer/security master itself bi-temporal -- store listing_status with [valid_from,valid_to), retain delisted/merged/bankrupt issuers, and snapshot the registry at ingest so 'as-of T' reconstructs the universe that EXISTED at T, not today's survivors. Every importance/lead-lag query must filter the universe by was-active-as-of-T, not just filter facts.

TRANSFER ENTROPY: Add a hard minimum-sample gate per pair and a power justification before any TE edge is emitted; prefer higher-frequency (intraday) returns where the data supports the estimator, or fall back to bivariate-conditioned Granger when sample size is insufficient. Subtract the surrogate-estimated finite-sample bias explicitly and report effect size + CI, not just FDR survival. Move the entire info-flow layer behind an explicit 'does this earn its complexity for 2 users?' gate.

TRUTH-DISCOVERY: Promote claim-canonicalization from openQuestion to a Phase-2 PREREQUISITE with its own gold set and accuracy bar; if cross-lingual claim-matching can't hit a defensible precision, run truth-discovery only WITHIN a language first and treat cross-lingual corroboration as a separate, lower-confidence signal rather than letting it silently inflate corroboration counts.
DROP SPLINK; use deterministic ID joins for the issuer master (the universe is bounded, as the doc itself argues) and reserve embedding-blocking + closed-set-LLM-abstain only for ambiguous residue.

NO-SIGNAL ENFORCEMENT: Stop calling it 'structural.' A directional FDR-surviving lead-lag edge IS a signal in substance no matter what vocabulary is banned; forbidding 'buy/sell' words is presentation policy, not structural prevention. Be honest in the doc that the no-signal guarantee is enforced at the presentation/ontology layer by NOT aggregating, and that the lead-lag layer is the one place that genuinely strains the GOAL -- which is the real reason to gate or drop it, not just phase it last.

REDDIT vs KR-PORTAL CONSISTENCY: The doc excludes Reddit on TOS/AI-training grounds while waving through 네이버/토스 portal scraping and Telegram capture, which carry comparable-or-worse TOS exposure. Either apply the compliance bar uniformly (and reassess the KR Tier-2 sources) or state explicitly why KR portal scraping is acceptable where Reddit is not -- right now it reads as an inconsistent rationalization.
